name: Your Trainer Workout (.ytw)
spec: The Your Trainer Workout format is a JSON document. Required top-level keys: programId (string), programName (string), description (string), totalDuration (integer seconds), and intervals (array). Optional: workoutType (POWER | HR_ZONE | ROUTE), variant (STANDARD | RAMP_TEST), difficulty (1-5), category, primaryLocale, and a strings localization block. Each entry in intervals is either a WorkoutInterval or a RepeatGroup. A WorkoutInterval has: duration (seconds), intensityZone (Z1-Z7 token), label, intervalType (WARMUP | COOLDOWN | INTERVAL), optional id, targetPowerPercent (integer % of FTP; for ramps this is the start), optional targetPowerEndPercent (ramp end; linear interpolation), optional targetHrZone (1-5 for HR_ZONE workouts), optional cadenceTarget (rpm), and optional cues (timed coaching messages: offsetSec, text, optional durationSec). A RepeatGroup has repeat (1-50) and intervals (WorkoutInterval list; nested repeat groups are not allowed); the app flattens it on import. The strings block maps each locale to {name, description, labels (keyed by interval id), cues (keyed by 'id:index')}. .ytw imports directly into Your Trainer.

examples.title: Interval with cues + localized strings

constraints.note: targetPowerPercent is an integer percentage of FTP (90 == 90% FTP); ramps interpolate to targetPowerEndPercent.
intervalType is WARMUP, COOLDOWN, or INTERVAL; repeat groups are flattened on import and may not nest.
Blocks referenced in strings.labels / strings.cues must carry a stable id; cues keys are 'id:index'.
RepeatGroup.repeat is 1-50; cue text best kept under 80 characters.

conversion_notes.note: Map intervalType WARMUP/COOLDOWN to Warmup/Cooldown, ramps (targetPowerEndPercent) to Ramp, constant blocks to SteadyState, and a 2-block repeat group to IntervalsT. Power: percent/100.
Encode targetPowerPercent as FIT %FTP (1000+percent); expand repeat groups; map intervalType to step intensity (warmup/cooldown/active).
Multiply targetPowerPercent by FTP/100 for absolute watts; sample ramps/repeats into minute,watt rows.

glossary.definition: WARMUP, COOLDOWN, or INTERVAL — drives display and work-only summaries.
Target power as an integer percentage of FTP (ramp start).
{repeat, intervals}; expands to repeat copies of the inner intervals on import.
Per-locale {name, description, labels, cues}; labels keyed by interval id, cues by 'id:index'.
